title: Desktop Calculator
tagline: A Windows desktop calculator built with C# and Windows Forms, featuring chained arithmetic operations, robust error handling, and intuitive calculator-style interaction.

features.title: Calculator-Style Input
features.desc: Implements an intuitive input flow that mirrors the behavior of a traditional desktop calculator.

features.title: Sequential Operations
features.desc: Supports chained arithmetic operations without requiring operator precedence, matching the behavior of the Windows Calculator.

features.title: Robust Error Handling
features.desc: Gracefully detects and handles invalid operations such as division by zero and integer overflow without crashing.

features.title: State-Based Calculation Engine
features.desc: Maintains calculation state between button presses, enabling continuous calculations and repeated equals operations.

context: Desktop Calculator is a Windows Forms application that recreates the functionality of a traditional desktop calculator. The project focuses on event-driven programming, state management, arithmetic processing, and responsive user interaction. Rather than evaluating mathematical expressions, the application processes user input incrementally, replicating the behavior of standard calculator software while ensuring reliable error handling and a consistent user experience.

tech.name: C#
tech.role: Implements the calculator engine, arithmetic operations, and application logic.

tech.name: .NET Framework
tech.role: Provides the runtime environment and standard libraries.

tech.name: Windows Forms
tech.role: Creates the graphical user interface and handles user interaction.

tech.name: Event-Driven Programming
tech.role: Processes button clicks and manages application state throughout calculations.

tech.name: Exception Handling
tech.role: Detects and handles invalid calculations such as overflow and division by zero.
